Title: METHOD AND APPARATUS OF ATOMIC FORCE MICROSCOPE BASED INFRARED SPECTROSCOPY WITH CONTROLLED PROBING DEPTH
Inventor: DAZZI, Alexandre; ROY, Anirban; YANG, Honghua
Kind: A1 - Published application with search report
Abstract: A method for obtaining optical spectroscopic information about a sub-micron region of a sample with quantitatively controlled depth/volume of the sample subsurface using a scanning probe microscope. With controlled probing depth/volume, the method can separate top surface data from subsurface optical/chemical information. The method can also be applied in liquid suitable for studying biological and chemical samples in their native aqueous environments, as opposed to air. In the method, a depth-controlled spectrum of the surface layer is constructed by illuminating the sample with a beam of infrared radiation and measuring a probe response using at least one of the resonant frequencies of the probe. The surface sensitivity is obtained by limiting the heat diffusion effect of the subsurface so as to confine the signal. The signal confinement is achieved through non-linearity of the acoustic wave with probe, as well as benefits gained by a high modulation frequency of the infrared radiation source at >1 MHz.L'invention concerne un procédé servant à obtenir des informations spectroscopiques optiques concernant une région submicronique d'un échantillon avec une profondeur/un volume qui sont contrôlés quantitativement de la sous-surface d'échantillon à l'aide d'un microscope à sonde à balayage. Avec la profondeur/le volume de sondage qui sont contrôlés, le procédé peut séparer des données de surface supérieure d'informations optiques/chimiques de la sous-surface. Le procédé peut également être appliqué dans un liquide approprié pour étudier des échantillons biologiques et chimiques dans leurs environnements aqueux natifs, par opposition à l'air. Dans le procédé, un spectre à profondeur contrôlée de la couche de surface est construit en éclairant l'échantillon au moyen d'un faisceau de rayonnement infrarouge et en mesurant une réponse de sonde à l'aide d'au moins l'une des fréquences de résonance de la sonde. La sensibilité de surface est obtenue en limitant l'effet de diffusion de chaleur de la sous-surface de manière à confiner le signal. Le confinement du signal est obtenu par la non-linéarité de l'onde acoustique par rapport à la sonde, et des avantages sont obtenus par une fréquence de modulation élevée de la source de rayonnement infrarouge à >1 MHz.

Title: Method for estimating the number of nuclei of a preselected isotope in a molecular species from an NMR spectrum
Inventor: 艾尔弗雷德·罗斯; ROSS ALFRED DR
Assignee: F·霍夫曼－拉·罗奇股份有限公司; HOFFMANN LA ROCHE
Kind: A - Unexamined application for a patent for invention
Abstract: 一种从含有分子物质作为主要成分的样品的NMR谱出发，估算所述分子物质中预定同位素的核数量的方法，所述NMR谱包含多个对应于所述核的信号峰。通过应用包含和排序标准得到一组有序信号峰，对其积分产生一组有序积分。进行嵌套的外部和内部迭代循环，其中对于每个循环，将一个尝试性的核数分配给所述有序积分中的一个，同时其它的有序积分相应地重新校正，并四舍五入得到下一个整数值，以产生候选的总核数。从具有最高出现次数的多个候选总核数中选择最小的一个，即得估计的总核数。The patent refers to the field of 'investigating or analysing materials by determining their chemical or physical properties'. A method for estimating the number of nuclei of a preselected isotope in a molecular species starts with an NMR spectrum of a sample containing said molecular species as a predominant substance, said NMR spectrum comprising a plurality of signal peaks corresponding to said nuclei. By applying inclusion and ordering criteria one obtains a set of ordered signal peaks, the integration of which leads to a set of ordered integrals. Nested outer and inner iteration cycles are carried out, wherein for each cycle a trial number of nuclei is assigned to one of said ordered integrals whilst the other ordered integrals are rescaled accordingly and rounded to the next integer value, so as to yield a candidate total number of nuclei. An estimated total number of nuclei is obtained by taking the lowest one from the plurality of candidate total numbers of nuclei that have the highest number of occurrences.

Title: Method of quantifying the formation and retention of hydrocarbons in source rock
Inventor: Salmon, Elodie; Lorant, Francois; Behar, Francoise
Kind: A1 - Application published with search report
Abstract: The method involves constructing a molecular model (MM) of a chemical macromolecular system by experimental characterizations of a sample of the system. A reaction scheme (SR) of heat maturation of the system is defined by submitting the model to a dynamic molecular simulation associated to a reactive force field. Hydrocarbons formed freely during the maturation are quantified by determining physico-chemical evolution (PC) of molecular mixture, directly defined by the scheme from the simulation, and by determining heat decomposition kinetics of the system by kinetics study (C) from the scheme. An independent claim is also included for a method for simulating genesis of a sedimentary basin.La méthode selon l'invention permet de modéliser la formation du pétrole et le phénomène de rétention dans la roche mère. Des expériences de caractérisation de la matière organique servent à établir le modèle moléculaire (MM) de l'échantillon de départ (E). La réaction de craquage thermique de ce modèle moléculaire est reproduite par des calculs de simulation moléculaire dynamique avec un champ de forces réactif (RMD) et validée par comparaison avec des données expérimentales. Le schéma réactionnel obtenu (SR) permet d'effectuer une étude cinétique (C) par variation du paramètre température. A partir de la simulation dynamique on détermine les équilibres de phases (PES) du milieu réactionnel à chaque instant. Les bilans successifs d'équilibres de phases à différents stades d'avancement de la réaction de craquage permettent de suivre l'évolution physico-chimique (PC) de la maturation thermique de l'échantillon organique étudié. Les hydrocarbures libres (liquides et gazeux), non retenus dans le résidu solide, sont quantifiables tout au long de la modélisation numérique de la maturation de l'échantillon, ils représentent, dans les basins sédimentaires, les hydrocarbures non retenus dans la matrice organique de la roche mère (Q). Cette quantité peut servir d'indicateur ou de valeur d'entrée comme seuil de rétention dans les modèles de bassin.

Title: A POROUS, MOLECULARLY IMPRINTED POLYMER AND A PROCESS FOR THE PREPARATION THEREOF
Inventor: SELLERGREN BOERJE; BUECHEL GUNTER
Assignee: MIP TECHNOLOGIES; SELLERGREN BOERJE; BUECHEL GUNTER
Kind: A1 - Published application with search report
Abstract: A porous, molecularly imprinted polymer and a process for its preparation are described. The porous, molecularly imprinted polymer is characterised in that it is obtainable by providing a porous silica; attaching a molecular template to the surface of the porous silica; filling the pores of the porous silica with a polymer; removing the silica and the molecular template, thereby leaving a porous, molecularly imprinted polymer. The process is characterised by the above defined process steps. Also described are a porous polymer vesicle and its preparation with the same features as defined for the porous, molecularly imprinted polymer and its preparation, except for the lack of the molecular template and thus the lack of the molecular imprint in the porous polymer.L'invention concerne un polymère poreux à empreinte moléculaire et un procédé de préparation associé. On obtient ledit polymère en fournissant une silice poreuse; en fixant à la surface de la silice poreuse un modèle moléculaire; en remplissant les pores de ladite silice avec un polymère; en évacuant la silice et le modèle moléculaire, ce qui laisse un polymère poreux à empreinte moléculaire. Le procédé se caractérise par les étapes définies ci-dessus. L'invention traite également d'une vésicule polymère poreuse et de sa préparation suivant les mêmes caractéristiques définies pour le polymère poreux à empreinte moléculaire et sa préparation, à l'exception de l'absence du modèle moléculaire et, par conséquent, l'absence de l'empreinte moléculaire dans le polymère poreux.

Title: TEST SYSTEM AND METHOD SUITABLE FOR SELECTING TEST MATERIALS AND FORMULATIONS
Inventor: LEIGH, Steve, PHARES Drug Delivery AG; LEIGH, Mathew Louis S., Phares Drug Delivery AG; VAN HOOGEVEST, Peter; TIEMESSEN, Henricus
Kind: A2 - Application published without search report
Abstract: The present invention provides a test system and a method for screening lipophilic and amphipathic materials with low water solubility. It generally relates to membrane lipid suspensions or dispersions employed as a test system for obtaining desired physico-chemical and biological information relating to the interaction of materials with membrane lipids in early screens in discovery. It serves as a selection method for derivatives of a test material for optimal membrane affinity and membrane permeation and thereby predict the potential for absorption in vivo. Furthermore, it may be used in later screening in formulation development, to select a carrier for a test material or derivative to enable the highest drug load. Title: Template induced patterning of surfaces and their reversible stabilization using phase transitions of the patterned material
Inventor: BAYERL THOMAS; DECHER GERO; BRAUNSCHWEIG THOMAS
Kind: A - Patent
Abstract: A two-dimensional patterning of a three-dimensional surface by a template molecule (or object) involving non-covalent interactions between the template and the surface and the reversible fixation of the pattern by undergoing a phase transition in the patterned material. In this invention the surface provides a multitude of mobile functional groups (matrix) which are spatially arranged by the interaction with the template, the template selects and arranges only those groups out of a multitude of functional groups which allow for a maximum of interaction between the template and the surface. The resulting pattern is then stabilized by reducing the mobility of all functional groups present on the surface by undergoing a phase transition to a state of high surface viscosity. After separation of the template and the patterned surface, the pattern on the surface is used for recognition, separation or catalytic purposes.

Title: Oil quality sensor
Inventor: Dickert, Franz; Forth, Peter; Lieberzeit, Peter; Voigt, Günter; Marquardt, Klaus Dieter
Kind: B1 - Patent without a pre-grant publication
Abstract: The chemical and/or physico-chemical determination of the ageing of a motor oil is very expensive and requires measuring methods which cannot be carried out on board a motor vehicle. The determination of only one oil condition parameter, for example the viscosity, only enables conditional statements to be made with respect to oil quality since conflicting effects may exist to varying degrees in this case. According to the invention, oil quality can be determined by a quartz base coated with a sensitive layer. The sensitive layer has a surface or volume which is adapted to an oil component and is suitable for the repeated incorporation and release of the oil component according to the concentration thereof. When the oil component is present, it is incorporated in the sensitive layer causing the resonant frequency of the layer to decrease via a mass effect or causing an effective increase of the component thickness or mass. As the oil ages, the proportion of the component incorporated in the sensitive layer decreases, so that the resonant frequency increases. A non-sensitive layer is used as a reference by which the viscosity effect of the oil on the oscillation of the quartz and the variation in viscosity of the motor oil are determined as a second important oil quality parameter.

Title: USE OF INORGANIC PARTICLES AND METHOD FOR MARKING AND IDENTIFYING A SUBSTRATE OR AN ARTICLE
Inventor: ROZUMEK OLIVIER; MUELLER EDGAR
Kind: A9 - Complete corrected document
Abstract: The present invention relates to inorganic particles comprising at least two chemical elements in a predefined and analytically identifiable ratio. These particles are used as a marking means incorporated into or applied onto any desired article. They provide a high security potential against counterfeiting since the analysis depend on a combination of spatial as well as of chemical information. In a first step the information containing particle has to be localized by scanning electron microscopy and in a second step the ratio of elements is analysed using energy- or wavelength-dispersive X-ray analysis (SEM/EDX).La présente invention concerne des particules inorganiques comprenant au moins deux éléments chimiques en un rapport prédéfini et identifiable par voie analytique. Ces particules sont utilisées comme moyen de marquage incorporé ou appliqué à n'importe quel article désiré. Elles permettent d'obtenir un potentiel de sécurité élevé contre la contrefaçon étant donné que l'analyse dépend d'une combinaison d'informations à la fois spatiales et chimiques. Dans une première étape, la particule contenant les informations doit être localisée par microscopie électronique à balayage et dans une seconde étape le rapport des éléments est analysé par analyse par rayons X à dispersion d'énergie - en longueur d'onde (SEM/EDX).

Title: Determination and uses of the atomic structures of ribosomes and ribosomal subunits and their ligand complexes
Inventor: Steitz, Thomas A.; Moore, Peter B.; Ban, Nenad; Nissen, Poul; Hansen, Jeffrey
Kind: B2 - Patent with a pre-grant publication
Abstract: The present invention provides methods for producing high resolution crystals of ribosomes and ribosomal subunits as well as the crystals produced by such methods. The x-ray diffraction patterns of the crystals provided by the present invention are of sufficiently high resolution for determining the three-dimensional structure of ribosomes and ribosomal subunits, for identifying ligand binding sites on ribosomes and ribosomal subunits, and for molecular modeling of ligands which interact with ribosomes and ribosomal subunits. Thus, the methods of the present invention may be used to produce ligands which are designed to kill or inhibit any specific target organism(s).

Title: Construction of molecular models, e.g. for drug design
Inventor: KRIEG BENNO PROF DR; FERINCZ JOZSEF DR
Assignee: KRIEG BENNO PROF DR; FERINCZ JOZSEF DR
Kind: A1 - Document laid open (first publication)
Abstract: Beschrieben wird ein Verfahren zur Ermittlung der 3-D-Geometrie von Molekülen. Der Aufbau eines Zielmodells bzw. Zielmodelldatensatzes erfolgt durch Verknüpfung von Molekülfragmenten, wobei die Umgebung der Verknüpfungsstellen zur Feinsteuerung herangezogen wird. Mit dieser neuen Technik können realitätsnahe Daten rasch und bequem erhalten werden, auch von solchen chemischen Verbindungen, deren Synthese und/oder Isolierung (noch) nicht gelungen ist. Die Leistungsfähigkeit des erfindungsgemäßen Verfahrens (hohe Genauigkeit, auch bei kostengünstigem Equipment/PC) liegt deutlich höher als die bisher beschriebener Methoden.Molecular model building system comprises successive addition of molecular model fragments. The fragments are designed with special consideration to the environment of the bonding position, to allow fine control of the coupling step. Pref. the fragments are pre-optimised and have non-exchangeable joints at their coupling sites. The fragments are held in a data bank for disposal and computer-assisted coupling stages are carried out. If necessary analogues found by a defined similarity principle are used.

Title: Molecular imprinting of solid-supported amphiphile layer capable of undergoing reversible phase transition
Inventor: BAYERL THOMAS PROF DR; DECHER GERO PROF DR; BRAUNSCHWEIG THOMAS DR
Kind: A1 - Document laid open (first publication)
Abstract: Die Erzeugung und Stabilisierung molekularer Abdrücke von Templaten in Oberflächen ist eine Methode zur selektiven Erkennung und Bindung von in einer mobilen Phase befindlichen Strukturen nach dem Schlüssel-Schloß-Prinzip mit Anwendung in den Bereichen Biotechnologie, Biochemie und Diagnostik. Wesentliche Nachteile bisheriger Methoden zur Erzeugung solcher Abdrücke sind die Verwendung von Quervernetzern (Polymerisation) zur Stabilisierung, die mangelnde Biokompatibilität der geprägten Oberflächen, die Irreversibilität einer einmal erzeugten Oberflächenprägung sowie Probleme des Stofftransports auf und innerhalb der Oberfläche.$A Ziel des neuen Verfahrens ist die Erzeugung und Stabilisierung dieser Oberflächenprägungen in reversibler Form, bei höchster Biokompatibilität und ohne die Verwendung von kovalenten Quervernetzungen.$A Ultradünne Schichten amphiphiler Moleküle (Monolayer oder Bilayer) werden an einen in wäßriger Phase befindlichen festen Träger gebunden. Ein Templat wird aus der wäßrigen Phase an die fluide Schicht angelagert. Dies bewirkt eine Umverteilung der unterschiedlichen amphiphilen Moleküle in der Schicht im Kontaktbereich zum Templat. Diese Verteilung wird durch einen reversiblen Phasenübergang in den quasi-kristallinen Zustand der Schicht fixiert. Anschließend wird das Templat entfernt. Die ultradünne Schicht erhält den molekularen Abdruck eines Teils der Templatkontur, bis sie wieder in den fluiden Zustand überführt wird und kann für molekulare ...Method for making a structured surface bearing the imprint of a template molecule using a mixture of different amphiphiles that can be induced to undergo a phase transition between a highly mobile liquid-like state (fluid state) and a solid-like crystalline state (gel or crystalline state) comprises coating a surface with the mixture in the fluid state, containing the coating with the template molecule, inducing the phase transition to the gel or crystalline state, and removing the template.

Title: Method for determining a temperature of a component

Kind: A1 - Document laid open (first publication)
Abstract: Das Verfahren zur Temperaturbestimmung eines Bauteils, beispielsweise einer Anfahr- oder Schaltkupplung im Getriebe eines Kraftfahrzeuges, besteht darin, daß die Temperatur des Bauteils mittels eines umfassenden, detaillierten Wärmeberechnungsmodells kontinuierlich berechnet wird und daß das Berechnungsmodell wärmetechnisch relevante Daten des Bauteils, dessen Umgebung sowie weitere Randbedingungen berücksichtigt. After calibration the heat-calculation model is reduced to a small number of component-relevant nodal points.

Abstract: Das Verfahren zur Temperaturbestimmung eines Bauteils, beispielsweise einer Anfahr- oder Schaltkupplung im Getriebe eines Kraftfahrzeuges, besteht darin, dass die Temperatur des Bauteils mittels eines umfassenden, detaillierten Wärmeberechnungsmodells kontinuierlich berechnet wird und dass das Berechnungsmodell wärmetechnisch relevante Daten des Bauteils, dessen Umgebung sowie weitere Randbedingungen berücksichtigt. Nach einer Kalibrierung wird das Wärmeberechnungsmodell auf wenige bauteilrelevante Knotenpunkte reduziert.The invention relates to a method for detecting the temperature of a component, for example a starting clutch or main clutch in the transmission of a motor vehicle. According to said method the temperature of the component is continuously calculated using a comprehensive, detailed heat-calculation model which takes into account heat-relevant data of the component in question, its environment and other secondary conditions. After calibration the heat-calculation model is reduced to a small number of component-relevant nodal points.L'invention concerne un procédé permettant de déterminer la température d'un élément, par exemple d'un embrayage de démarrage ou d'un embrayage principal de la transmission d'une automobile. Selon ce procédé, la température de l'élément est calculée en continu au moyen d'un modèle détaillé et circonstancié de calcul de la chaleur. Ledit modèle de calcul prend en compte des données significatives concernant l'élément en question, sur le plan thermique, son environnement, ainsi que d'autres conditions secondaires. Après étalonnage, le modèle de calcul de la chaleur est réduit à quelques points nodaux propres à l'élément concerné.

Abstract: The present invention provides methods for producing high resolution crystals of ribosomes and ribosomal subunits as well as the crystals produced by such methods. Title: Determining intracellular degradation of cell-permeable substance, useful e.g. for optimizing properties of vaccinating peptides, by measuring concentration of degradation products
Inventor: Brock, Roland, Dr.rer.nat., 72074 Tübingen, DE; Fischer, Rainer, 72070 Tübingen, DE; Waizenegger, Thomas, 81245 München, DE
Kind: A1 - Document laid open (first publication)
Abstract: Die Erfindung betrifft ein Verfahren zur Bestimmung der intrazellulären Degradation einer zellpermeablen Substanz, das besonders geeignet ist, die Grundlage für die Optimierung der pharmakokinetischen Eigenschaften von pharmazeutischen Wirkstoffen zu schaffen. Darüber hinaus wird eine pharmazeutische Zusammensetzung beschrieben, die als Wirkstoff eine zellpermeable Substanz aufweist, die keiner oder einer wesentlichen intrazellulären Degradation unterliegt, ein Verfahren zur Herstellung einer derartigen pharmazeutischen Zusammensetzung sowie ein Verfahren zur Verbesserung der pharmakokinetischen Eigenschaften einer zellpermeablen Substanz. Außerdem wird ein Verfahren zum Auffinden von Inhibitoren der intrazellulären Degradation von zellpermeablen Substanzen und ein Verfahren zur Bestimmung des Mechnismus der intrazellulären Degradation einer zellpermeablen Substanz beschrieben (Fig. 1).Determining intracellular degradation of a cell-permeable substance (I) comprises adding (I) to a medium being used to grow cells and measuring the accumulation of degradation products (II) of (I). Independent claims are also included for the following: (1) pharmaceutical composition comprising (I) that has been shown by the new method to undergo insignificant or significant intracellular degradation; (2) improving the pharmacokinetic properties of (I) by elucidating the structure responsible for degradation and then modifying the point of attack within this structure by molecular modeling; (3) identifying inhibitors (III) of intracellular degradation of (I) by including a test inhibitor in the growth medium; and (4) determining the mechanism of intracellular degradation of (I) by adding a known inhibitor, characterized by substrate specificity, to the test medium.

Title: Method of quantifying hydrocarbon formation and retention in a source rock
Inventor: Salmon, Elodie; Lorant, Francois; Behar, Francoise
Kind: B2 - Patent with a pre-grant publication
Abstract: The method according to the invention allows modelling of oil and the retention phenomenon in the source rock. Organic matter characterization experiments are used to establish the molecular model (MM) of the initial sample. The thermal cracking reaction of this molecular model is reproduced by dynamic molecular simulation computations with a reactive force field and validated by comparison with experimental data. The reaction mechanism permits a kinetic study by variation of the temperature parameter. The successive phase equilibrium assessments at various progress stages of the cracking reaction allow following the physicochemical evolution of the thermal maturation of the organic sample. The free hydrocarbons are not retained in the solid residue can be quantified throughout numerical modelling of the sample maturation.

Title: System and methods for integrated and predictive analysis of molecular, imaging, and clinical data for patient-specific management of diseases
Inventor: Comaniciu, Dorin; Mansi, Tommaso; Lim, Wei Keat; King, Vanessa; Kremer, Andreas; Georgescu, Bogdan; Zheng, Xudong; Kamen, Ali; Keller, Andreas; Staehler, Cord Friedrich; Wirsz, Emil
Kind: B2 - Patent with a pre-grant publication
Abstract: A system operating in a plurality of modes to provide an integrated analysis of molecular data, imaging data, and clinical data associated with a patient includes a multi-scale model, a molecular model, and a linking component. The multi-scale model is configured to generate one or more estimated multi-scale parameters based on the clinical data and the imaging data when the system operates in a first mode, and generate a model of organ functionality based on one or more inferred multi-scale parameters when the system operates in a second mode. The molecular model is configured to generate one or more first molecular findings based on a molecular network analysis of the molecular data, wherein the molecular model is constrained by the estimated parameters when the system operates in the first mode. The linking component, which is operably coupled to the multi-scale model and the molecular model, is configured to transfer the estimated multi-scale parameters from the multi-scale model to the molecular model when the system operates in the first mode, and generate, using a machine learning process, the inferred multi-scale parameters based on the molecular findings when the system operates in the second mode.

Title: PRECIPITATED SILICA HAVING A HIGH STRUCTURE AND PROCESS FOR PRODUCING IT
Inventor: NAUROTH PETER; ESCH HEINZ; TURK GUNTER DR
Assignee: DEGUSSA-HUELS AG, 60311 FRANKFURT, DE; DEGUSSA AG, 40474 DUESSELDORF, DE
Kind: D1 - Granted European number in bulletin
Abstract: There are disclosed precipitated silicas which are characterized by the following physical-chemical data: -A B C -BET-surface area m2/g 400-600 400-600 400-600 -according to DIN 66131 -DBP-Number % 340-380 320-360 310-360 -Bulk density according g/l 180-220 75-120 35-70 -to DIN 53194 -'ALPINE-Sieve Wt. % 25-60 63 m mu - Depending on the particle distribution curve, these precipitated silicas can be employed as silica carriers for active materials of all types, as antiblocking agents for polypropylene and polyethylene films, as thickening silica in specific polar systems in which pyrogenic silicas show little thickening capacity, as highly active matting agents for varnishes and as catalyst carriers as well as insulating materials. The precipitated silicas are produced by establishing a silica final concentration of 46 grams/l in a receiver having water present heated to a temperature of 40 DEG -42 DEG C. while holding the pH constant in the range of 6-7 by simultaneously running in a water glass solution and sulfuric acid while maintaining shearing with an Ekato Turbine over the entire precipitation time, interrupting the precipitation for 90 minutes from the 13th to 103rd minute according to a total precipitation of 146 minutes, aging the suspension of precipitated silica for 12-17 hours, separating the precipitated silica from the suspension with the help of a filter press, washing, liquifying the filter cake with water and/or acid to a suspension having 10-16 weight % solids, finally spray drying and either not grinding or grinding with a cross flow mill or jet mill.

Title: SYSTEM AND METHODS FOR INTEGRATED AND PREDICTIVE ANALYSIS OF MOLECULAR, IMAGING, AND CLINICAL DATA FOR PATIENT-SPECIFIC MANAGEMENT OF DISEASES
Inventor: COMANICIU, Dorin; MANSI, Tommaso; LIM, Wei, Keat; KING, Vanessa; KREMER, Andreas; GEORGESCU, Bogdan; ZHENG, Xudong; KAMEN, Ali; KELLER, Andreas; STAEHLER, Cord, Friedrich; WIRSZ, Emil
Assignee: SIEMENS; MANSI TOMMASO; LIM WEI KEAT; KING VANESSA; KREMER ANDREAS; GEORGESCU BOGDAN; ZHENG XUDONG; KAMEN ALI; KELLER ANDREAS; STAEHLER CORD FRIEDRICH; WIRSZ EMIL; COMANICIU DORIN
Kind: A3 - Separate publication of the search report
Abstract: A system operating in a plurality of modes to provide an integrated analysis of molecular data, imaging data, and clinical data associated with a patient includes a multi-scale model, a molecular model, and a linking component. The multi-scale model is configured to generate one or more estimated multi-scale parameters based on the clinical data and the imaging data when the system operates in a first mode, and generate a model of organ functionality based on one or more inferred multi-scale parameters when the system operates in a second mode. The molecular model is configured to generate one or more first molecular findings based on a molecular network analysis of the molecular data, wherein the molecular model is constrained by the estimated parameters when the system operates in the first mode. The linking component, which is operably coupled to the multi-scale model and the molecular model, is configured to transfer the estimated multi-scale parameters from the multi-scale model to the molecular model when the system operates in the first mode, and generate, using a machine learning process, the inferred multi-scale parameters based on the molecular findings when the system operates in the second mode.L'invention concerne un système fonctionnant dans une pluralité de modes pour permettre une analyse intégrée de données moléculaires, de données d'imagerie et de cliniques associées à un patient. Le système comprend un modèle multi-échelle, un modèle moléculaire et un composant de liaison. Le modèle multi-échelle est configuré pour générer un ou plusieurs paramètres multi-échelle estimés, sur la base des données cliniques et des données d'imagerie lorsque le système fonctionne dans un premier mode, et pour générer un modèle de fonctionnalité organique, sur la base d'un ou de plusieurs paramètres multi-échelle inférés lorsque le système fonctionne dans un deuxième mode. Le modèle moléculaire est configuré pour générer un ou plusieurs premiers résultats moléculaires, sur la base d'une analyse de réseau moléculaire des données moléculaires, le modèle moléculaire étant limité par les paramètres estimés lorsque le système fonctionne dans le premier mode. Le composant de liaison, qui est fonctionnellement accouplé au modèle multi-échelle et au modèle moléculaire, est configuré pour transférer les paramètres multi-échelle estimés du modèle multi-échelle vers le modèle moléculaire lorsque le système fonctionne dans le premier mode, et pour générer, en utilisant un processus d'apprentissage machine, les paramètres multi-échelle inférés sur la base des résultats moléculaires lorsque le système fonctionne dans le deuxième mode.

Title: MOLECULAR MODELS
Kind: A - Patent specification
Abstract: 1502637 Structural joints A DREIDING 4 March 1975 [5 March 1974] 8847/75 Heading F2M [Also in Divisions E2 and G5] Relates to stereochemical models of molecules made up from a number of structural elements 20 each representing a polyvalent atom and/or atom group and for this purpose having two or more connector arms 22a, 22b, 22c representing the valencies of an atom or atom group. In order to allow quick coupling and release of each arm from an arm of an adjacent structural element or, where as shown the arms are flexible, from another arm of the same element, each arm has at its free end a coupling element 24 adapted to mate directly with an identical coupling element on any other arm of the or another structural element. Preferably, in addition, each arm is rotatable relative to a central body 21 of the element by a coupling 23 consisting of a spigot 29 which fits in a socket 28 of the body 21 and is grooved to receive a spring retaining clip 31, 32. The base of the groove in the spigot may have a number of flats (42, Fig. 3A or 43, Fig. 3B, not shown) to define preferred rotational positions of the spigot within the socket. In one form the coupling elements 24 each consist of an insert 36, Fig. 4, which is pushed into a closely coiled spring 26 constituting one of the arms, e.g. arm 22a, the insert being formed to provide a projecting spigot 37 of semi-circular section having a rib 39 and a groove 40, the insert being pushed into the coiled spring such that a complementary socket 38 is formed. The resilience of the spring 26 allows the spigot of a similar coupling element on another arm 22d to be pushed into the socket 38 so that its rib 39 snaps into the groove 40 while rib 39 of the spigot 37 snaps into the groove 40 of the other spigot. In the modification of Fig. 7 the rib 39 is omitted and groove 40 is replaced by a circumferential groove 50 which is engaged by an end portion 49 of the coiled spring of the other arm 22d. A leaf spring (58, Fig. 9, not shown) may replace the portion 49. A coupling element 24 incorporating a rotational coupling similar to coupling 23 may be used (Fig. 10, not shown). In Fig. 15 each coupling element has a pair of hooks 89, in addition to a spigot and socket of semicircular section, so that the coupling is locked by bayonet action. In Fig. 16 a magnetic coupling is used.

Title: Thermostable coating system for hexa:cyano-benzene on pure oxide free silicon - used as passivation, insulation, sub-coat, surface modifier, organic semiconductor or signal determn. in silicon substrate
Inventor: KARL NORBERT PROF DR; MOEBUS MANFRED; GOEPEL WOLFGANG PROF DR; SCHMEISSER DIETER DR
Kind: A1 - Document laid open (first publication)
Abstract: Das organische aromatische Akzeptormolekül Hexacyanbenzol (HCNB) wurde auf Si(111) abgelagert. Wachstumsmodus und elektrische Struktur von HCNB wurden mittels Röntgen- und UV-Photoelektronenspektroskopie (XPS und UPS) untersucht. Dünne Filme im Submonoschicht- und Monoschichtbereich wurden mittels einer massenspektrometrisch gesteuerten Sublimationstechnik hergestellt. Es wurde gefunden, daß HCNB unter allen Expositionsbedingungen auf nicht disoziative Art adsorbiert wurde. Die Daten erlauben es, zwischen drei verschiedenen Adsorptionsbereichen zu unterscheiden. Die anfängliche Adsorption führt zu bevorzugt paralleler Ausrichtung der HCNB-Moleküle mit einem starken, durch die Wechselwirkung mit dem Substrat induzierten Dipolmoment. Die Austrittsarbeit nimmt um 1,7 eV ab. In zweiter Schicht werden die Moleküle nicht parallel adsorbiert und unterliegen starker Wechselwirkung mit jenen der ersten Schicht. Weitere Exposition führt zum Wachstum dreidimensionaler Cluster schwach gebundener Moleküle, die bei Temperaturen über 200°C desorbieren. Bei diesen Temperaturen reagieren die ausgerichteten Moleküle der ersten zwei Schichten chemisch und bilden eine außerordentlich stabile Überschicht, die bis etwa 600°C bestehen bleibt. Bei noch höheren Temperaturen wird Si-Carbidbildung, also Fragmentation, beobachtet. Im Gegensatz dazu werden auf SiO 2 -bedeckten Siliciumsubstraten nur physikalisch adsorbierte Moleküle beobachtet, die um 200°C desorbieren.Thermostable coating system is claimed obtd. by applying hexacyanobenzene (I) to a pure, oxide-free Si surface (II). Mfr. of this involves (a) prepg. a smooth (II); (b) removing the oxide film and other surface contamination from (II) by heating in vacuo above 900 deg.C for several seconds; (c) cooling (II) to under 60 deg.C, pref. with liquid N2; (d) vapour deposition of (I) in a thickness of over 1, pref. 2-10 nm at under 10 power(-4), pref. under 10 power(-7) Pa; and (e) heating slowly to 100-600, pref. 150-250 deg.C at a rate of ca. 20 deg.C/min. to cause chemical bond formation. USE/ADVANTAGE - The system is used as surface passivation film; electrical insulation; subcoat for other coatings; surface modification; organic semiconductor, opt. in combination with the underlying semiconductor; and determn. of electrical, optical, thermal or chemical data signals in the underlying semiconductor (claimed). Thin film in the sub-monolayer and monolayer range can be produced. The first (I) layer is chemisorbed on (II) with parallel orientation, whilst the second is adsorbed by the first at a different angle and further growth gives 3D clusters of weakly bound mols. Heating converts the first two layers to a very thermostable polymeric film, whilst the clusters are desorbed above 200 deg.C. In contrast, only physisorption occurs on SiO2 and (I) readily sublimes off on heating to ca. 200 deg.C.

Title: Method for charging particles suspended in gases
Inventor: SCHMIDT-OTT ANDREAS; SIEGMANN HANS-CHRISTOPH
Kind: A - Patent
Abstract: The invention concerns a method and apparatus for electrically charging particles of liquid or solid matter suspended in gases, especially in air. The particle carrying gas is irradiated with ultraviolet light having an energy below the threshold for ionization of the gas, but above the photoelectric threshold of the particles. The actual charging occurs by photoemission of electrons from the particles. The photoelectrons or negative small ions are removed from the neighborhood of the positively charged particles by diffusion to a charge absorbing surface. The photoelectric charging method and apparatus of the present invention are highly effective, particularly for very small particles and yields chemical information on the particles and their surface.

Title: Modules for molecular models consisting of models of molecular fragments and bonding elements which assume a minimum energy conformation after being combined
Inventor: KARFUNKEL,HEINRICH,DR.
Kind: A1 - Document laid open (first publication)
Abstract: Modules for molecular models having statistic and dynamic similarity with real system of the microcosm. Molecular models for non-covalent interacting chemical species, which are constructed in a modular fashion from bonding elements and models of molecular fragments, reproduce relevant intermolecular and intramolecular forces true to scale and spontaneously assume a minimum energy confrmation are the subject-matter of the invention. The bonding elements (chemical bonds) chiefly reproduce the dependence of the torsional forces on the dihedral angle. Non-bonding interactions are simulated by spatially fixed magnets and movable multipole caps. In this case, the fixed magnets reproduce the electrostatic interactions and the movable multipole caps the dispersion forces, true to scale. The 'short-range' repulsive forces are realised by elastically compressible surfaces. In the standard version, the components have a specific weight which is equal to that of a liquid. Gravitation forces are eliminated due to immersion in said liquid, and temperature effects can be represented qualitatively by means of turbulence. In the cosmic version, the masses of the components are constructed true to scale, resulting in dynamic similarity. The main field of application of these models is large molecular systems which are not accessible by other means for the purpose of predictions.
